title: Chunked Transfer Coding, Extensions, Trailers, and Decoding · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs chunked transfer coding, extensions, trailers, and decoding from bounded byte and state evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the incremental chunked decoder and trailer policy. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for incremental chunked decoder and trailer policy after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Parse bounded hexadecimal chunk size with extensions, overflow checks, exact CRLF, and cumulative decoded limits.
Read exactly each chunk-data length plus its terminating CRLF across arbitrary fragmentation.
Treat the last zero-sized chunk as the transition to trailer parsing rather than immediate message completion.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Chunk Size Hex Overflow
steps.instruction:
Order the Chunk Size Hex Overflow evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Chunk Size Hex Overflow.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Chunk Size Hex Overflow assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Chunk Size Hex Overflow, keep Chunk Size Hex Overflow connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: A review protocol team handles case 5145 in a canary edge service advertising HTTP/3 with bounded fallback. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound chunk accumulation and require trailer validation after the zero chunk; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then saturate one stream window while another remains independently serviceable. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Parse bounded hexadecimal chunk size with extensions, overflow checks, exact CRLF, and cumulative decoded limits.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Chunk Size Hex Overflow has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
